title: gynandropsis decipiens
intro: Gynandropsis decipiens, also known as "false holly," is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gynandropsis decipiens include treating fever, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as having potential antimalarial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical applications, and it can also be used as a spice or added to soups and sauces.
